name: Ronnie Cuber
abstract: Ronald Edward Cuber (December 25, 1941 – October 7, 2022) was an American jazz saxophonist. He also played in Latin, pop, rock, and blues sessions.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: we did one with three baritones, Cecil Payne and Ronnie Cuber... I just did one three months ago with three baritones — Ronnie Cuber, Gary Smulyan and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: So basically in that band the drummer was Larry Rosen... Eddie Daniels, Ronnie Cuber a baritone player, myself...
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: Saxes: George Young, Lou Marini, Michael Brecker, Eddie Daniels, Ronnie Cuber baritone sax. Rhythm section: Steve Gadd, Tony Levin on bass, Richard Tee on piano, Hugh McCracken on guitar. We did sixteen takes in a three hour session.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Of the people who are around now, it’s just Eddie Gomez, baritone saxophonist Ronnie Cuber, and myself, and Michael Benny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: when I was on Woody Herman’s band playing with Ronnie and Sal Nistico and Frank Vicari, man that was a delight.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: There’s the Roulette record... Live At Laren in Holland from seventy-nine with a New York band. Red Rodney, John Eckert, Jimmy Knepper, Sam Burtis, Ronnie Cuber, Bennie Aranov, Ray Drummond, and Billy Hart.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
